prompt: Which platform mechanisms are used to track and escalate incidents that are not being resolved according to organizational standards?
choices: Service level agreements (SLAs) and the inactivity monitor
MID Server discovery schedules and software license counters
Change conflict detection rules and CAB approval records
CMDB identification and reconciliation scheduled jobs
explanation: Incident Management uses SLAs to monitor progress against agreed targets and the inactivity monitor to generate events when records go without updates.

prompt: An administrator must enable the Create Child Incident UI action on the incident form. Which configuration is required?
choices: Activate the Enable copy incident feature property only
Select the Enable create child incident feature (com.snc.incident.create.child.enable) incident property
Set glide.ui.autoclose.time to zero on the System Properties page
Install the Major Incident Management Taxonomy Content Pack plugin
explanation: Copy and create-child capabilities are controlled by incident properties at Incident > Incident Properties, including com.snc.incident.create.child.enable.

prompt: When a fulfiller uses Create Child Incident from an active parent incident, which field is copied to the new record by default?
choices: The Closed by field from the parent incident
The resolution code after the parent is Resolved
Category
The major incident communication plan assignment
explanation: Default copied fields include category, subcategory, business service, configuration item, impact, urgency, assignment group, and related lists such as affected CIs.

prompt: When should an implementation team classify an incident as a major incident according to ITSM guidance?
choices: When the incident results in significant business disruption or affects many users
When any caller opens a self-service password reset request
Whenever the incident is assigned to the default Service Desk group
Only after a related problem record reaches the Known Error state
explanation: Major incidents are high business-impact events that require a response beyond routine incident management, such as critical service outages or widespread user impact.

prompt: A service desk agent believes an open incident requires accelerated major incident handling. Which action proposes the record as a major incident candidate?
choices: Convert the incident to a Normal change request from the Related Records tab
Click Propose Major Incident from the incident form context menu
Manually run the Autoclose Incidents scheduled job for the record
Set the incident state to Closed and create a new catalog task
explanation: Agents can propose an incident as a major incident candidate using the Propose Major Incident context menu action before a major incident manager promotes it.
